free_text: New data and evaluation of 3He-induced nuclear
Inst. of Nucl. Res. Debrecen, Hungary
Lawrence Livermore Nat. Lab., Livermore, Ca
Inst. of Physics and Power Engineering,
China Nucl. Data Cent., China Inst. of
High purity, (>99%) natCu foils of 8.4 and 10 microns
Monitor foils: High purity (>99%)  Ti (10 micron) from
used. Stacks were irradiated with 3He beams up to
36 MeV. Beam current was monitored by Ti foils and
measured in Faraday-cup. Irradiation lasted 30 min.
Cumulative elemental production cross sections were
measured. The energy of the primary beam was determined
by TOF in Juelich and calibrated magnetic deflection in
from Table of radioactive isotopes,p.123-3, Wiley,
TS: Reaction SF8 and SF9 in Subents 003,
004, 006, 008 was corrected according to new rules.
SD: REACTION code updated according to new
EXFOR rules in Subent 003, 005, 007 and 009.  Small
corrections in all Subents. REL-REF added.
degradation was calculated using tables from this
The uncertainty of the primary beam energy was
+-0.2 MeV. The energy attenuation was calculated by
using the polynomial approximation of Andersen (see
REL-REF) along the stacks. The mean energy uncertainty
varies from +-0.2 to +-1.4 MeV along the stacks.
Total error of the data was about 12% to 13%.
SD: SF9=DERIV added to REACTION code.
